---
title: "Research Activities"
format: html
---

::: {.lead}
Our group specializes in the continuum mechanics of soft, living, and active materials. By bridging experimental characterization, advanced bioimaging, and state-of-the-art computational modeling, we tackle critical challenges in physiology, mechanobiology, and tissue engineering.
:::

::: {.d-flex .flex-wrap .gap-2 .mb-5 .mt-4}
<a href="#cornea" class="btn btn-outline-primary mb-2">👁️ Cornea Mechanics</a>
<a href="#eye" class="btn btn-outline-primary mb-2">👁️‍🗨️ Ocular Mechanics</a>
<a href="#clot" class="btn btn-outline-primary mb-2">🩸 Blood Clots</a>
<a href="#hydrogel" class="btn btn-outline-primary mb-2">🧫 Hydrogels & Biomaterials</a>
<a href="#others" class="btn btn-outline-primary mb-2">🔬 Soft Matter & Methods</a>
:::

---

<div id="cornea" style="padding-top: 2rem;"></div>
::: {.grid .align-items-center .mb-5}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
![](resources/images/members/Placeholder.png){.rounded .shadow-sm width="100%"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
### Corneal Mechanics & Refractive Surgery

We investigate the intricate lamellar microstructure of the cornea to predict its response to surgical interventions and degenerative diseases. Our approach combines advanced experimental testing (bulge tests, Optical Coherence Tomography, and 3D Digital Volume Correlation) with custom, microstructure-informed finite element models to characterize anisotropy, structural gradients, and collagen remodeling.

**Key topics:** [Corneal Anisotropy]{.badge .border .border-secondary .text-secondary .mb-1} 
[OCT & DVC Imaging]{.badge .border .border-secondary .text-secondary .mb-1} 
[Refractive Surgery Simulation]{.badge .border .border-secondary .text-secondary .mb-1}

[📚 View Relevant Publications](publications.html?search=Cornea){.btn .btn-sm .btn-primary .mt-3}
:::
:::

<hr class="text-muted">

<div id="eye" style="padding-top: 2rem;"></div>
::: {.grid .align-items-center .mb-5 .mt-4}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
![](resources/images/members/Placeholder.png){.rounded .shadow-sm width="100%"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
### Full Ocular Mechanics & Imaging

Beyond the cornea, we model the mechanical environment of the entire eye globe. This includes utilizing AI-based segmentation on MRI data to generate patient-specific meshes of the eye and extraocular muscles. By simulating muscular contraction, we characterize the influence of eye movements on long-term ocular growth, remodeling, and myopia development during childhood.

**Key topics:** [Extraocular Muscles & Myopia]{.badge .border .border-secondary .text-secondary .mb-1} 
[AI MRI Segmentation]{.badge .border .border-secondary .text-secondary .mb-1} 
[Growth & Remodeling]{.badge .border .border-secondary .text-secondary .mb-1}

[📚 View Relevant Publications](publications.html?search=Eye){.btn .btn-sm .btn-primary .mt-3}
:::
:::

<hr class="text-muted">

<div id="clot" style="padding-top: 2rem;"></div>
::: {.grid .align-items-center .mb-5 .mt-4}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
![](resources/images/members/Placeholder.png){.rounded .shadow-sm width="100%"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
### Mechanics of Blood Clots & Hemostasis

Understanding the mechanics of thrombus formation and dissolution is crucial for tackling cardiovascular diseases. By coupling solid mechanics with advanced optical tools (biophotonics), we characterize the 3D microstructural architecture and microrheology of fibrin networks. Our multiscale approach aims to predict clot stability and embolization risks under physiological conditions.

**Key topics:** [Thrombus Visco-hyperelasticity]{.badge .border .border-secondary .text-secondary .mb-1} 
[Microrheology]{.badge .border .border-secondary .text-secondary .mb-1} 

[📚 View Relevant Publications](publications.html?search=Clot){.btn .btn-sm .btn-primary .mt-3}
:::
:::

<hr class="text-muted">

<div id="hydrogel" style="padding-top: 2rem;"></div>
::: {.grid .align-items-center .mb-5 .mt-4}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
![](resources/images/members/Placeholder.png){.rounded .shadow-sm width="100%"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
### Synthetic Hydrogels & Smart Biomaterials

Hydrogels are essential tools for mechanobiology and tissue engineering. We study cell-matrix interactions—such as how Smooth Muscle Cells orient and remodel the Extracellular Matrix—to understand the biophysics of conditions like Thoracic Aortic Aneurysms. Our team also develops magneto-responsive gels and 3D/4D printed shape-morphing polymers for advanced biomedical scaffolds and bone fixation.

**Key topics:** [Cell-Matrix Remodeling]{.badge .border .border-secondary .text-secondary .mb-1} 
[Collagen Polymers]{.badge .border .border-secondary .text-secondary .mb-1} 
[Mechanical modelling]{.badge .border .border-secondary .text-secondary .mb-1}

[📚 View Relevant Publications](publications.html?search=Hydrogel){.btn .btn-sm .btn-primary .mt-3}
:::
:::

<hr class="text-muted">

<div id="others" style="padding-top: 2rem;"></div>
::: {.grid .align-items-center .mb-5 .mt-4}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
![](resources/images/members/Placeholder.png){.rounded .shadow-sm width="100%"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
### Soft Matter Mechanics & Advanced Numerical Methods

We push the boundaries of computational mechanics to solve complex physiological problems. This includes poromechanical modeling of skin and skeletal muscle, inverse problems for cardiovascular hemodynamics, and the development of hybrid Reduced-Order Models (PGD) coupled with Deep Learning to provide real-time solutions for highly parameterized, large-strain mechanics problems.

**Key topics:** [Inverse Problems & Parameter Estimation]{.badge .border .border-secondary .text-secondary .mb-1} 
[Poromechanics]{.badge .border .border-secondary .text-secondary .mb-1} 
[Reduced-Order Modeling]{.badge .border .border-secondary .text-secondary .mb-1}

[📚 View Relevant Publications](publications.html?search=Others){.btn .btn-sm .btn-primary .mt-3}
:::
:::
